differential_diagnoses: Penetrating duodenal ulcer with duodenocaval or duodeno-renal fistula
Streptococcus anginosus group bacteremia secondary to occult intra-abdominal abscess
next_steps: Repeat CT imaging of the abdomen (optimized/gated) or MRCP to better characterize the duodenal-renal interface
Upper endoscopy to evaluate for duodenal pathology or fistula
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotic coverage targeting Streptococcus anginosus group
Optimize anticoagulation management for the IVC/renal vein thrombosis
Repeat antiphospholipid panel after the acute phase to confirm or rule out secondary vs. primary APS
